refs.note: Cicero refers back to his own consulship (63 BC) as the necessity that has called him out of philosophical otium.
Cato's saying about being never less alone than when alone is also recalled in De Officiis 3.1; Cicero attributes it here through Cato's report of Africanus.
Reference back to Polybius and Panaetius as Scipio's authorities for the mixed constitution.
Scipio refers back to the threefold typology of states in book 1.
On the fragility of the kingly form, refers back to book 1's treatment of Cyrus.
On the threefold kinds of constitutions; Scipio refers Tubero back to book 1.
``As I said yesterday'': Scipio refers to book 2's account of the corrupt forms of state.
The Somnium, by recalling Africanus's military career, connects back to the discussion of empire and justice in book 3.
